metrics.narrative: Jai Rastogi has articulated concrete pain in dollar and time terms (a cheese spend report takes 'two weeks' to compile via manual spreadsheets, 29 May 2026 call; 'it would take months' to build cross-category consumption/pricing analytics, 29 May 2026 call). On 3 Jun 2026 the demo surfaced illustrative impact figures (an estimated $126k-$300k in potential dairy consolidation savings; a cited 600% ROI from an existing Zycus customer in spend insights). These are Zycus-provided estimates and reference-customer figures, not yet a Domino's-specific validated business case — that validation is exactly what the pending Spend POC is meant to produce.

sources: Avoma call 2026-07-03 (RE: DPE x Zycus - Platform Overview / POC Focus)
champion.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group Chief Procurement & Supply Chain Officer) is driving the whole evaluation: he initiated contact after meeting Zycus at a conference (Next_Step__c, 22 Apr 2026 entry), is 'aligning/coaching' his own team ahead of the demo (5 May 2026), is personally coordinating data scope and category priorities (3 Jul 2026 call), and offered to broker further executive access ('I'll make sure that we have a connect when he's back in town', 29 May 2026 call). He is expanding the deal's scope himself ('extend to CLM this year', 22 Apr 2026) and has invited additional finance, legal, and European stakeholders into the process. This reads as strong, active advocacy, though his access to the economic buyer (CFO) has not yet translated into her direct engagement.

competition.narrative: No active competitor is named for this evaluation (Salesforce Competitor field is blank, consistent with the read). Jai Rastogi mentioned personal familiarity with SAP Ariba, Coupa, and iValua on the 29 May 2026 call ('I've worked with everyone from iValue to Cooper to Ariba') and stated plainly 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone' — this is prior-role experience (he referenced reviewing Sirion/Icertis-type CLM tools 'when I was at Thai Union'), not a live bake-off at Domino's. The deal currently reads sole-source, which is a positive but unverified-durable signal given no formal RFP has been run.

identify_pain.narrative: Deeply and repeatedly articulated by Jai Rastogi across two calls: procurement maturity is 'exceptionally low... bodies in spreadsheets' (29 May 2026); no common taxonomy across disparate ERPs; a cheese spend rollup takes two weeks; no CLM system, contracts held in disparate repositories with 'significant contract leakage'; indirect spend (travel, marketing, technology) is 'completely unmanaged' by procurement and tracked informally by EAs and via Jira (29 May and 3 Jun 2026 calls). This is confirmed, buyer-voiced, and specific.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact (MSA, Order Form, SOW, redline) has surfaced in any source read this sweep. The deal is pre-POC at Formal Evaluation, and Jai Rastogi's own framing is 'in-principle agreement to award post-POC' (29 May 2026) — paper process genuinely has not started.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (Lynn Carruthers, CFO, Executive Sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) holds the Executive Sponsor contact role in Salesforce, and the 3 Jun 2026 call confirmed a 'Head of Finance to review POC outcomes for integration decision' — i.e. finance sign-off is the acknowledged gate. However, Lynn Carruthers has not appeared or been quoted on any of the three read calls (29 May, 3 Jun, 3 Jul 2026); Jai Rastogi is currently the sole conduit to finance. The EB is identified but not yet directly engaged.

decision_process.narrative: The process is buyer-stated: demo -> data-driven POC on Domino's own extracts -> internal signoff -> an in-principle agreement to award (29 May 2026 Next Step). The 3 Jun 2026 call added detail: the Head of Finance reviews POC outcomes for the integration decision, and the Chief Legal Officer separately assesses the CLM component. What is not yet confirmed is the formal governance chain beyond the POC (who signs, what board/committee, if any) since the economic buyer herself has not weighed in directly.

sources: Avoma call 2026-07-03 (RE: DPE x Zycus - Platform Overview / POC Focus)
decision_criteria.narrative: Jai Rastogi laid out explicit priority order on 29 May 2026: (1) spend visibility and sourcing-opportunity identification on Domino's own data, (2) a CLM system to centralize contracts and cut compliance leakage, (3) building out the full source-to-contract environment from there. The 3 Jul 2026 call further narrowed the near-term criterion to a working, own-data POC on iAnalyze plus ANA against indirect tail spend, with a suggestion to start with one limited category (packaging) to make the test achievable.

items.summary: Zycus (Luke Dougherty, Shirley Islam) walked Domino's (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler, Antje Radüchel-Harders, Dani de Jong) through the platform, agreeing the POC will start with iAnalyze spend analysis before moving to Anna for tactical low-dollar negotiations; three months of indirect spend data was requested as the initial input, with Zycus handling normalization pending Domino's validation.

items.name: Shirley's post-meeting recap and materials
items.summary: Shirley Islam sent Domino's the POC scope materials and the three templates reviewed on the call (iAnalyze first, Anna to follow), asking for an initial data cut on chosen indirect categories to begin iterative normalization; she also flagged the Anna negotiation export will follow on Monday, leaving Domino's to respond with data and category scope.

items.name: Scheduling thread that set up the call
items.summary: Included in the thread is the prior back-and-forth where Michael Gengenbach (new European lead, unable to attend the original demo) proposed Friday 8.30-9.30 CET as an alternative since he's on holiday from the following week, which Zycus accommodated — explaining why the meeting landed on this date.

items.name: Duplicate meeting record with same scheduling email
items.summary: A second logged instance of the same scheduling thread/event description, reflecting calendar/system logging of the rescheduled call rather than new substantive activity.

day_summary.overall: The rescheduled platform overview finally happened, with Zycus walking Domino's European team (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler and others) through the POC scope, confirming it will start with iAnalyze spend insights before layering in Anna for tactical negotiations. Zycus followed up same-day with the promised templates and materials, and the ball is now in Domino's court to check what indirect spend data (Navision/ERP extracts) they can actually pull and confirm scope, likely narrowed to a category like packaging.

cro_panel.intro: An early-stage but genuinely engaged deal; a strong, active champion (Jai Rastogi) has run two full discovery/demo sessions and expanded the buying committee into Europe, and a buyer-initiated email reply on 2 Jul 2026 confirms two-way engagement is current.; But the economic buyer (CFO) and the CIO influencer have never appeared on a call, no competitor is contesting the deal, and the gating deliverable for the buyer's own stated award process (a clean data extract for the POC) is already showing friction just as the close-date runway compresses

items.detail: POC depends on Domino's delivering clean, usable data extracts from 12-13 disparate ERP systems (Navision, D365, local ERPs) with no common taxonomy, messy supplier names, and limited category mapping. Jai flagged 6 Jul 'some concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources'. 3 Jul call confirmed data extraction is uncertain and Domino's must check Navision/ERP formats and advise feasibility. If data pull stalls or is too messy for Zycus to normalize, POC cannot run and momentum dies.

CFO (Lynn Carruthers, Economic Buyer) has not directly engaged and is passive. Jai Rastogi (CPO) is coaching finance stakeholders to build the internal ROI case via the POC, but CFO approval is not locked. If POC results are thin or the ROI narrative is weak, CFO may defer investment given cost-pressure context (share price fell, transformation funding is tight). Jai stated 'if we see some real tangible results using our data... there'll be a lot of momentum' — the inverse is also true: no tangible results = no momentum.

Wayne McMahon (CIO, Influencer contact role) is mapped but not engaged. POC involves ERP data pulls, integration scoping, and SaaS onboarding; CIO alignment is critical for InfoSec review, data governance, and IT approval during contracting. If CIO surfaces late with objections (security, integration complexity, competing IT priorities), contracting could stall post-POC.

ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) French-language support unconfirmed. Maria Oehler (Domino's Germany) asked 3 Jul whether ANA can communicate with French vendors in French; Zycus answer pending. If ANA is English-only, POC scope may need to narrow to English-speaking markets (Australia, some Europe) or exclude certain suppliers, which could reduce demonstrated savings and weaken the ROI case.

POC execution timeline is compressed: 52 days to close (28 Aug), and POC has not yet launched (awaiting Domino's data extracts, overdue per 6 Jul). If data delivery slips 2-3 weeks, POC runs in late Jul, results land early Aug, leaving minimal time for CFO approval, contracting, legal/InfoSec review, and signature by 28 Aug. Close date may slip to Q3/Q4 2026 if POC or contracting takes longer than planned.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is genuinely live: a two-way buyer email reply landed 7 days ago (2 Jul 2026) and a multi-market POC-scoping call followed the next day (3 Jul 2026). This is not a cooling or dark deal, but the gating deliverable for the buyer's own award process is showing early friction.

north_star_verdict.math: Today is 8 Jul 2026; close date is 28 Aug 2026 = 51 days out. Opp created 22 Apr 2026 (~11 weeks in Formal Evaluation). Sequence to date: demo request (5 May) -> demo scheduled (26 May) -> alignment call (29 May) -> full platform demo (3 Jun) -> POC discussion (9 Jun, not recorded) -> POC planning continuing (22 Jun) -> Europe POC-scoping call (3 Jul) -> Next Step update flagging DPE resourcing concerns (6 Jul). The POC (data extraction, normalization, execution) plus CFO/EB review plus an 'in-principle' award plus contracting all still lie ahead of a 51-day runway; realistically this points to a Q4 2026 close, not 28 Aug.

north_star_verdict.headline: Sole-source, champion-led evaluation is genuinely live (buyer email 2 Jul 2026, Europe POC-scoping call 3 Jul 2026), but the POC itself has not launched and 51 days to the 28 Aug 2026 close cannot absorb data normalization across 12-13 ERPs, CFO review, and contracting.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (unchanged category; recommend moving Close Date out to Q4 2026 to reflect real POC/contracting lead time)

flags: Economic Buyer (CFO Lynn Carruthers) has not engaged directly; Jai is coaching finance stakeholders but CFO approval is passive, not active. Secure CFO touch post-POC results.
CIO (Wayne McMahon) not engaged; InfoSec/IT approval process unknown. CIO alignment critical for SaaS onboarding and ERP integration during contracting.
POC data extraction overdue and uncertain (6 Jul 'some concerns', 3 Jul 'we've got to check with our Navision team'). If data delivery slips 2-3 weeks, POC timeline compresses and close date at risk.
No quantified savings target or ROI threshold locked; POC results must hit an unstated bar to unlock CFO approval.
No competitor intel; sole-source eval assumed but not confirmed. If POC results are weak, CFO may request multi-vendor comparison.
No competitive RFP is running (Jai: 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone', 29 May 2026) - lock a directional in-principle commitment while sole-sourced, before any market process opens.

Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Executive Sponsor) and Wayne McMahon (CIO, Influencer) remain unengaged on every read call; get one direct touchpoint with Lynn Carruthers before POC results land so budget approval isn't a cold ask.

POC scope has both expanded (AU to Germany/Netherlands) and needs narrowing per the 3 Jul 2026 recommendation to a single starter category (e.g. packaging) - locking this now will speed a demonstrable win ahead of the 28 Aug close date.

Bring Lynn Carruthers into POC progress reviews now, not after results land: Jai is building the internal case alone, and a cost-pressured CFO (Domino's share price fell $161 to $16.68) will need direct visibility to release budget.

Protect the sole-source position by moving fast: no formal RFP is running and Jai has explicitly ruled out shopping the market (29 May 2026); a slow POC creates room for internal governance to force a competitive process.
Domino's spend data spans 12-13 disparate ERPs with no common taxonomy; offering hands-on Zycus data-normalization help now is the single highest-leverage move to get the POC (and the win) unstuck.

The CFO holds the budget but has never engaged directly; getting her into an early POC results review protects against SF's own flagged risk that internal politics could reopen the field to Coupa or iValua.

Jai Rastogi is a strong, multi-threading champion who brought in Domino's Europe unprompted; keep him tightly coached with a realistic POC timeline and a sharp ROI story to sustain the momentum he is building.

Sole-source status is fragile until the POC lands tangible results: Jai warned 'if we see some real tangible results... there'll be a lot of momentum' (29 May 2026) — prioritize a fast, narrow POC (packaging category, per the 3 Jul 2026 Europe suggestion) over a broad multi-market pull to protect that momentum.

Data-normalization risk across 12-13 disparate market ERPs threatens the POC's credibility; scope the first pass to Australia's cleaner data plus one Europe market to guarantee a crisp proof point before expanding further.
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage lever right now is protecting the champion's own timeline: unblock the data extract before it erodes the 50-day runway, and close the two open product questions (French-language ANA support, POC category scoping) that are currently sitting with Zycus.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage but genuinely engaged deal: a strong, active champion (Jai Rastogi) has run two full discovery/demo sessions and expanded the buying committee into Europe, and a buyer-initiated email reply on 2 Jul 2026 confirms two-way engagement is current. But the economic buyer (CFO) and the CIO influencer have never appeared on a call, no competitor is contesting the deal, and the gating deliverable for the buyer's own stated award process (a clean data extract for the POC) is already showing friction just as the close-date runway compresses.

deal_risk.text: Data-extraction risk: Domino's runs 12-13 disparate market ERPs (Navision, D365, local systems) with no common taxonomy or consistent supplier naming (3 Jul 2026 call), and Jai Rastogi already flagged execution concerns on 6 Jul 2026.
Timeline compression: 50 days remain to the 28 Aug 2026 close and the POC — the buyer's own precondition for an in-principle award — has not started.

win_position.text: Jai Rastogi told Zycus directly on 29 May 2026 'you've got a front run... I haven't gone out to market to anyone,' a buyer-voiced preference signal with no active competing vendor named.
The champion is proactively expanding scope (CLM, multiple European markets) and brokering further executive access, per the 22 Apr, 29 May, and 3 Jul 2026 notes.
The economic buyer, Lynn Carruthers (CFO), has not appeared on any read call; her review of POC outcomes was named as the actual integration-decision gate on 3 Jun 2026, and she remains unengaged.
The buyer's own award process is POC-gated and the POC has not started; Jai Rastogi flagged execution concerns on 6 Jul 2026, just days after the data ask was made on 3 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer email reply landed on 2 Jul 2026, and a POC-scoping call with an expanded European buying committee followed on 3 Jul 2026.

The most recent Next Step update (6 Jul 2026) is a rep-logged status note awaiting Domino's data delivery, not a fresh confirmed buyer action; there is no verified buyer touch since the 2 Jul 2026 email.

customer_commitment.text: Jai Rastogi has personally coached his internal team ahead of each demo (5 May, 29 May 2026) and pulled in finance, legal, and multiple European markets unprompted.
Commitment from the economic buyer and CIO is unproven; both are mapped contact roles (CFO Executive Sponsor, CIO Influencer) with zero call presence to date.

This is a direct buyer-side ask: CPO Jai Ratongi is explicitly driving an evaluation for ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent) with a mini-POC to prove quick value to internal stakeholders, with CLM as Phase 2.
• On 5 May 2026: 'Spoke to Jai who has asked for a demo to his team as next steps- he is aligning/coaching his team and will revert back to us early May.' On 11 May 2026: 'we're in contact and working on a date late May.' Jai is actively coaching his team for a demo and pushing the eval cycle fast ‚Äî classic AI-champion behaviour, not a passive evaluation. The opp name is literally 'Domino's ANA' and the product scope is iContract; ANA (ANA being the AI/autonomous negotiation product).
• Confidence is High. Domino's is a named-CPO-driven ANA POC with a defined Phase 2 extension to CLM. Caveat: BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC (despite Jai's active championship ‚Äî possibly a data lag), and the formal demo to Jai's team hasn't happened yet. Score 8 reflects buyer-led AI demand with a champion but pending demo execution; would move to 9 with a successful POC.

Jai Rastogi (CPO) is coaching finance stakeholders to build the internal ROI case via the POC, but CFO approval is not locked. If POC results are thin or the ROI narrative is weak, CFO may defer investment given cost-pressure context (share price fell $161 → $16.68, transformation funding is tight). Jai stated 'if we see some real tangible results using our data... there'll be a lot of momentum' — the inverse is also true: no tangible results = no momentum.
